note: Auditable view-coverage decision table for the caddy (task 8). Each candidate area is scored on (1) installation complexity and (2) difficulty of seeing it from the primary whole-model views, then decided ZOOM or a recorded WHY-NOT. Audited BOTH directions: a high-complexity, poorly-visible area with no zoom and no why-not is a finding; a redundant zoom (mirror/simple/well-visible) is over-generation and is a why-not here. Renders: scripts/render_caddy_views.py -> outputs/armchair_caddy/views/.
context_treatment: The sofa arm is a role:existing context body that OCCLUDES the subject (finding C1); the zoom variants (g1/g2/g3) are rendered with the arm HIDDEN so the joinery reads.

primary_views.shows: whole caddy saddling the arm (arm occludes; see g1_iso arm-hidden)
straddle across the arm, cup-hole edge, both sides hanging free with clearance
cup-hole opening + the four screw stations

candidate_areas.rationale: a bored full-thickness notch — a fabrication op with interior geometry; the top primary shows the opening, the zoom shows the bore through the 1in board.

candidate_areas.area: +X butt joint / 2-screw cluster into end grain
candidate_areas.rationale: highest local density — a fabrication + fastening spot (2 screws, RailCapScrewed) — AND fully occluded by the arm in every oblique primary; needs an arm-hidden zoom.

candidate_areas.area: -X butt joint / 2-screw cluster into end grain
candidate_areas.rationale: mirror-identical to the +X joint about the Y-Z plane (a SymmetricAbout invariant would prove the reflection); one representative joint zoom suffices. A second identical zoom would be over-generation.

candidate_areas.area: arm-clearance fit (side board vs arm, 0.25in gap)
candidate_areas.rationale: tolerance/fit-sensitive (the saddle drops on and lifts off by this gap); the thin gap is not readable in the whole-model primaries.

candidate_areas.area: top-board <-> arm gravity bearing (the saddle contact)
candidate_areas.rationale: a single flush bearing plane, no fabrication; the FRONT and END primaries already show the flush seating on the arm. A dedicated zoom would add nothing.
a plain square crosscut end, fully visible in FRONT/ISO; no fabrication, fit, or fastening complexity — a zoom would be over-generation.

candidate_areas.area: screw-tip embedment into the side end grain (interior)
candidate_areas.rationale: interior geometry with no section/cutaway capability in this render path; the ~1in embedment is VALIDATED (hardware-presence PASS + allowlisted screw-into-wood overlap), not drawn. Recorded as an honest render limitation, not a fabricated cutaway.
